subtasks.description: Query MongoDB SAM database to retrieve Slack contract records with renewal dates, license counts, contract terms, and renewal status
Query SQL warehouse to extract Slack usage consumption trends over time, including active users, message volume, and feature utilization metrics

Reconcile contract renewal dates and license allocations from SAM against consumption trends; analyze trajectory alignment, identify over/under-utilization, and recommend renewal posture (renew, negotiate, reduce, optimize)

plan.cost_rationale: 3 subtasks, minimum decomposition: (1) MongoDB SAM query for contract state (mid tier—requires aggregation across contract records), (2) SQL consumption query for trend analysis (mid tier—likely requires time-series aggregation), (3) cross_recon to reconcile both sources and generate renewal trajectory recommendations (frontier tier—requires multi-signal reasoning, posture classification, and forward-looking trajectory analysis across heterogeneous data). No policy_action added because renewal recommendations and posture classification are part of cross_recon reconciliation, not separate governance approval. No multitool_plan needed because the query does not require orchestration of the enterprise tool registry.

output.sql: SELECT event_date, SUM(daily_active_seats) AS active_seats, SUM(amount_usd) AS total_spend, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS active_users, COUNT(*) AS event_count FROM consumption_facts WHERE product ILIKE '%slack%' AND event_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY event_date ORDER BY event_date

reconciled.reconciliation_notes: Cannot execute without actual data. Framework requires: (1) MongoDB contract records with renewal_dates and allocated_seats, (2) SQL consumption time-series for utilization trending

required_analysis.trajectory_alignment: Compare month-over-month active_seats growth against contract renewal cycle
required_analysis.utilization_ratio: (actual_active_seats / allocated_seats) - flag >90% as at_risk, <50% as over_provisioned
required_analysis.spend_trend: Analyze total_spend trajectory vs contract_value for negotiation baseline
required_analysis.user_adoption: Track active_users growth to validate seat allocation assumptions

recommended_classifications.renew: utilization 75-95%, positive trend, growth trajectory justifies current allocation
recommended_classifications.negotiate: utilization 85%+, negotiation upside if usage continues; seek volume discount
recommended_classifications.reduce: utilization <40%, declining trend, recommend seat reduction at renewal
recommended_classifications.optimize: utilization 50-75%, stable; consolidate licenses or negotiate tiered pricing
recommended_classifications.at_risk: utilization >95% or spike in event_count with declining seat availability

output.execution_note: Provide actual MongoDB and SQL query results to complete reconciliation. Expected MongoDB output: array of contract documents. Expected SQL output: time-series dataframe with event_date, active_seats, total_spend, active_users, event_count columns.
